Package org.eventb.core.ast

Class Type

java.lang.Object
org.eventb.core.ast.Type
Direct Known Subclasses:
BooleanType, GivenType, IntegerType, ParametricType, PowerSetType, ProductType, TypeVariable
public abstract class Type extends Object
Common protocol for event-B types.

In event-B, once type-checked, every expression has an associated type. This type statically determines the set of all values the expression can take. Hence, the type of an expression corresponds to the largest set to which the expression can belong.

For implementation reasons, types are not represented as expressions but have their own set of classes.

This class is not intended to be subclassed or instantiated directly by clients. To create a new type, clients should use the methods provided by the FormulaFactory class.

Since:
1.0

  • Method Details

    • buildExpression

      protected abstract Expression buildExpression(FormulaFactory factory)

    • buildString

      protected abstract void buildString(StringBuilder buffer)

    • equals

      public abstract boolean equals(Object obj)
      Indicates whether the given object denotes the same type as this one.

      Comparison is done using deep equality, not reference equality.

      Overrides:
      equals in class Object
      Parameters:
      obj - the object with which to compare
      Returns:
      true if this type denotes the same type as the obj argument, false otherwise

    • hashCode

      public abstract int hashCode()
      Overrides:
      hashCode in class Object

    • isSolved

      public boolean isSolved()
      Returns whether this type is non-generic (doesn't contain any type variables).
      Returns:
      true iff this type is non-generic

    • getFactory

      public FormulaFactory getFactory()
      Returns the formula factory used to build this type.
      Returns:
      the formula factory used to build this type
      Since:
      3.0

    • ensureSameFactory

      protected void ensureSameFactory(Type[] types)
      Ensures that the formula factory of each of the given types is the same as the formula factory of this type. Throws an IllegalArgumentException otherwise.
      Parameters:
      types - types to check
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if any of the given types has a different formula factory
      Since:
      3.0

    • ensureSameFactory

      protected void ensureSameFactory(Type left, Type right)
      Ensures that the formula factory of each of the given types is the same as the formula factory of this type. Throws an IllegalArgumentException otherwise.
      Parameters:
      left - type to check
      right - type to check
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if any of the given types has a different formula factory
      Since:
      3.0

    • ensureSameFactory

      protected void ensureSameFactory(Type other)
      Ensures that the formula factory of the given type is the same as the formula factory of this type. Throws an IllegalArgumentException otherwise.
      Parameters:
      other - type to check
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if any of the given types has a different formula factory
      Since:
      3.0

    • toExpression

      public Expression toExpression()
      Returns the expression that denotes the set corresponding to this type.
      Returns:
      the set corresponding to this type
      Since:
      3.0

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object

    • getSource

      public Type getSource()
      Returns the source type of this type, if any.

      If this type looks like ℙ(alpha × beta), this method will return alpha, otherwise null.

      Returns:
      the source type of this type, or null

    • getTarget

      public Type getTarget()
      Returns the target type of this type, if any.

      If this type looks like ℙ(alpha × beta), this method will return beta, otherwise null.

      Returns:
      the target type of this type, or null

    • isRelational

      protected boolean isRelational()
      Returns whether this type is a relational type.

      This type is relational if it looks like ℙ(alpha × beta).

      Returns:
      true iff this type is a relational type.

    • getBaseType

      public Type getBaseType()
      Returns the base type of this type, if any.

      If this type looks like ℙ(alpha), this method will return alpha, otherwise null.

      Returns:
      the base type of this type, or null.

    • addGivenTypes

      protected abstract void addGivenTypes(Set<GivenType> set)

    • getGivenTypes

      public final Set<GivenType> getGivenTypes()
      Returns a set of all given types which are used in this type.

      Since 3.0 this type does not need anymore to be solved for using this method. If the type is not solved the method will return given types only for solved parts of the type. The type must be unsolved only during the type checking phase.

      Returns:
      a set containing all given types which are used in this formula types
      Since:
      2.6

    • specialize

      public final Type specialize(ISpecialization specialization)
      Returns the type obtained by applying the given specialization to this type. Applying a specialization to a type has the effect of replacing all given types occurring in this type by their substitute as defined by the given specialization.

      The given specialization may change as a side-effect of calling this method. Please see the documentation of ISpecialization for details.

      If the specialization does not have any effect on this type (i.e., no given type gets substituted), this method returns this type rather than a copy of it.

      Parameters:
      specialization - the specialization to apply
      Returns:
      a specialization of this type or this if unchanged
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the given specialization is not compatible with this type, that is the specialization contains a substitution for an identifier with the same name as a given type in this type, but with a different type
      Since:
      2.6

    • accept

      public abstract void accept(ITypeVisitor visitor)
      Accepts the visit of this type with the given simple visitor.
      Parameters:
      visitor - the visitor to call back during traversal
      Since:
      2.7

    • isTranslatable

      public boolean isTranslatable(FormulaFactory ff)
      Tells whether this type can be translated to the given factory.

      This type is translatable if and only if:

      • All the given types it contains are compatible with the given factory (their names are not reserved keywords);
      • All parametric types it contains are supported by the given factory.

      Returns:
      true iff this type can be translated to the given factory
      Since:
      3.0
      See Also:
      • translate(FormulaFactory)

    • translate

      public Type translate(FormulaFactory ff)
      Returns a copy of this type built with the given formula factory.

      If the factory of this type and the given factory are equal, then this type is returned. Otherwise a new type object is built.

      Clients must ensure that this type can be translated to the given factory, prior to calling this method. If it is not translatable, an exception is raised.

      This operation is not supported by TypeVariable

      Parameters:
      ff - the factory to use to build a copy of this type
      Returns:
      a type equivalent to this type and built with the given factory
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if this type cannot be translated to the given factory
      UnsupportedOperationException - if this type contains a type variable
      Since:
      3.0
      See Also:
      • isTranslatable(FormulaFactory)
